data.title: Bank Cyprus reports FY results
data.description:
• Bank Cyprus press release (OTCPK:BKCYF): FY GAAP EPS of €1.14.
  • NII at €822 mn up 4% yoy; 4Q2024 NII down 3% qoq to €198 mn, mainly reflecting the decline in interest rates
  • Total operating expenses2 up 8% yoy to €367 mn, due to higher staff costs, IT, marketing and professional fees
  • Cost to income ratio2 remains low at 34%; 4Q2024 cost to income ratio2 at 38% largely due to quarterly seasonally higher expenses
  • Profit after tax of €508 mn up 4% yoy, of which €107 mn in 4Q2024

data.title: France inflation rises more than expected to 1.7% in January
data.description:
The inflation rate in France increased to 1.70% in January from 1.30% in December, above estimates of 1.4%.

The Consumer Price Index in France increased 0.10% in January over the previous month.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: European futures point to higher open as U.S.-Russia peace talks set to begin
data.description:
European futures rose overnight as peace talks between the U.S. and Russia begin in Saudi Arabia, a day after expectations of increased military spending pushed the region's defense stocks to all-time highs on Monday.

Euro Stoxx 50 futures were up 0.1% overnight. The pan-European STOXX 600 (STOXX) ended Monday 0.5% higher, led by defense stocks, as European leaders called for higher defense spending at an emergency meeting in Paris.

The Stoxx 600 Aerospace and Defense index, a gauge for the region's defense stocks, rose 4.6% to a record closing high on Monday. Germany's Renk Group +16.6% and Rheinmetall +14%, Sweden's Saab +16.2%, the U.K.'s BAE Systems +7.9%.

French President Emmanuel Macron called an emergency meeting of European leaders on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump arranged peace talks with Russia, excluding European allies and Ukraine. U.S. and Russian officials have convened in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday for negotiations.

Trump's decision forced European leaders to accept that they would have to do more to support Ukraine. However, at the emergency meeting, the leaders were split on deploying peacekeepers to Ukraine, given the risks of a potential direct confrontation with Russia and stretching European military capabilities.

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he had a long conversation with France's Macron. "We share a common vision: security guarantees must be robust and reliable. Any other decision without such guarantees - such as a fragile ceasefire - would only serve as another deception by Russia and a prelude to a new Russian war against Ukraine or other European nations."

data.title: UK's unemployment rate steady at 4.4%
data.description:
UK's unemployment rate held steady at 4.4% from October to December 2024, lower than estimates of 4.5%.

This remains the highest level since the three months ending in May, driven by an increase in individuals unemployed for up to 12 months.

Meanwhile, the number of employed individuals rose by 107K to 33.86M, with a Y/Y growth in full-time and part-time employees. The number of people with second jobs also increased, now representing 3.8% of all employed individuals.

The economic activity rate edged lower to 21.5% from 21.6% in the preceding period.

ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: KKR acquires stake in Employment Hero from SEEK Investments
data.description:
• KKR (NYSE:KKR) to acquire a stake in employment management solutions company Employment Hero from SEEK Investments, it said on Monday.
  • The transaction is expected to be completed by the first quarter of 2025.
  • Employment Hero has surpassed A$250 million in annual recurring revenue.

data.title: Antofagasta reports FY results
data.description:
• Antofagasta press release (OTC:ANFGF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $62.80.
  • Revenue of $6.61B (+4.6% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • Continued strong safety performance, with no fatalities and the lost time injury frequency rate continuing at a level below 1.0.
  • Revenue increased by 5% to $6.6 billion, reflecting the higher copper price, partly offset by lower sales volumes due to the rescheduling of vessels between periods following adverse weather conditions(sea swells) during December 2024 in the north of Chile.
  • EBITDA was $3.4 billion, 11% higher on stronger revenues and robust cost control, which helped to increase the Group’s EBITDA margin2 to 52%.
  • Cash flow from operations increased by 8% to $3.3 billion, with the same drivers as described above.

data.title: Exodus Movement says offer to acquire Banxa Holdings has expired
data.description:
Exodus Movement (NYSE:EXOD) Monday said that its previously announced offer for the acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding common shares of Banxa Holdings (TSXV:BNXA:CA) has expired without reaching an agreement with Banxa.

Exodus will remain responsible stewards of capital with a disciplined approach to acquisitions.
